title: Quantization_map (nucleosome-lattice property) ranks #1 in random-forest permutation importance for crossover prediction; GC ranks 8th

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'Quantization_map (nucleosome-lattice property) ranks #1 in random-forest permuta'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: Permutation importance ranking of 24 biophysical features from the random forest M1 model in exp17_formal_claim. Table at results/a6_feature_importance.csv.

operations.note: Add rank column via descending sort on permutation_importance.

statistics.name: rank_1_feature_by_permutation_importance
statistics.note: Layer-2 nucleosome-lattice property (Polymer_Evolution phase 3 output), not a composition descriptor.

statistics.name: gc_content_permutation_rank
statistics.note: GC ranks 8th of 24 features. Composition is sub-dominant to arrangement (Layer 2 nucleosome lattice).

statistics.name: top_5_features_by_importance
statistics.note: All five top features are arrangement-dominated or nucleosome-mechanical. None is a pure composition descriptor.

inference.justification: Single conjunct: GC content ranks below the top 5 features. This licenses the 'arrangement beats composition for crossover prediction' framing. Note: the parent claim (exp17_formal_claim) establishes that biophysics-minus-GC outperforms biophysics-with-GC; this child claim drills into *which* biophysics features carry the GC-independent signal. The string-valued s_top_feature stat is reported but not used in the inference; equality on a string is fragile under future feature renaming.
inference.failure_mode: If GC ranked in the top 5, the 'arrangement dominates composition' framing would weaken — though the parent ΔAUROC argument would still hold. A GC rank of 1 would fully invert the conclusion but is contradicted by the parent claim's M2 > M1 result.

conclusion.assertion: In the random forest M1 model (24 biophysical features predicting top-quartile crossover hotspots at 1 Mb autosomal resolution), permutation importance ranks quantization_map (a Layer-2 nucleosome-lattice property) at #1, with GC content ranking 8th. The top 5 features are all arrangement-dominated or nucleosome-mechanical; no pure composition descriptor reaches the top 5. Layer-2 properties dominate composition for predicting recombination.

scope.scale_note: Single-model feature ranking. Cross-validation of the ranking (rerun with different seeds / different RF backends) is a queued sibling claim.

confidence.note: Permutation importance is well-defined; per-rank stability under repeat training is the natural confidence statistic but not yet computed.

external_assumptions.statement: Permutation importance (not Gini) is the appropriate ranking metric for correlated features; Gini importance would inflate ranks for tracks correlated with the dominant signal carrier.
quantization_map (Polymer_Evolution phase 3 output) is a defensible operationalization of nucleosome lattice discretization; the metric was pre-registered before the RF was run.
